title: Oracle HCM 26B Integration Options
audience: IT steering committee and HR leadership

slides.headline: Oracle HCM 26B Integration Options
slides.speaker_notes: Welcome the steering committee. Today we present three integration approaches for Oracle HCM 26B and our recommendation.

slides.headline: What We'll Cover
slides.speaker_notes: Five sections. The comparison matrix on slide 9 is the key decision input.

items: 26B release context and new API coverage
Positions-vs-Jobs model migration
Three integration options deep-dive
Side-by-side evaluation matrix
Recommendation and next steps

slides.headline: 26B Introduces 14 New REST Endpoints
slides.speaker_notes: The new endpoints cover position management, workforce structures, and absence management. This reduces our custom extract dependency from 80% to under 30%.

items: 14 new REST endpoints for position management
API coverage rises from 45% to 82% of HR data
Three legacy extract jobs eligible for retirement

content.alt_text: Diagram showing Oracle HCM 26B API coverage expansion

slides.headline: Positions vs Jobs Requires Migration
slides.speaker_notes: This is a one-time migration but it blocks all three integration options. The migration script has been tested in sandbox but not in production.

items: 26B restructures org hierarchy: positions are now first-class entities
One-time migration of 12,000 job records to position model
Downstream payroll and benefits integrations require re-mapping

slides.headline: Three Integration Options
slides.speaker_notes: Moving into the core analysis. Each option was evaluated by the integration team over the past three weeks.

content.tagline: Full API, HCM Extract, and Hybrid — evaluated on cost, risk, and timeline

slides.headline: Option A: Full API — Lowest Cost, Highest Effort
slides.speaker_notes: Full API gives us real-time data but the 12-week build is the longest path. Rate limiting at 500 req/min is manageable for our volume.

items: Real-time position sync via REST, no batch lag
12-week implementation for custom position orchestration
Lowest ongoing cost: $8K/year infrastructure

slides.headline: Option B: HCM Extract — Fastest, 24h Lag
slides.speaker_notes: HCM Extract is Oracle's out-of-box solution. Four-week deploy, but the 24-hour lag on position changes means managers see stale org charts.

items: Batch file extracts deployed in 4 weeks
Up to 24-hour data freshness lag on position changes
Moderate ongoing cost: $15K/year due to storage and scheduling

slides.headline: Option C: Hybrid — Best Balance
slides.speaker_notes: The hybrid approach uses real-time API for position data (which changes frequently) and batch extract for payroll (which is inherently periodic). This matches the natural cadence of each data type.

items: Real-time API for positions, batch extract for payroll
8-week implementation: API first, extract parallel
Best long-term flexibility for future 27A features

slides.headline: Hybrid Wins on 4 of 5 Criteria
slides.speaker_notes: The only criterion where hybrid loses is initial timeline — 8 weeks vs 4 for extract. But the 4-week gap is recovered within the first year through lower maintenance.

slides.headline: Recommend Hybrid — Approve by April 30
slides.speaker_notes: If we get approval today, the integration team starts May 1. The org migration runs in parallel during May. Target go-live is aligned with Oracle's August 26B GA window.

items: Proceed with hybrid: API for positions, extract for payroll
Begin org migration May 1, target August go-live
Budget: $11K/year ongoing, $45K one-time implementation
